User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Hatid application, either a rider or a driver.

displayName.description: The display name of the user.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the user.

Driver.title: Driver
Driver.description: Represents a driver using the Hatid application, extending the User entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the driver.  Also a reference to the User entity.

availability.description: Indicates whether the driver is currently available to accept ride requests.

rating.description: The average rating of the driver.

Rider.title: Rider
Rider.description: Represents a rider using the Hatid application, extending the User entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the rider.  Also a reference to the User entity.

rating.description: The average rating of the rider.

Trip.title: Trip
Trip.description: Represents a ride trip within the Hatid application.

startLocation.description: The starting location of the trip (e.g., coordinates or address).

endLocation.description: The ending location of the trip (e.g., coordinates or address).

startTime.description: The start time of the trip.

endTime.description: The end time of the trip.

scheduledTime.description: The time the trip was scheduled for. Will be null if the trip wasn't scheduled

routeOptimizationEvents.description: Json block of the different optimization events that occurred during the trip.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Represents a vehicle used by a driver in the Hatid application.

make.description: The make of the vehicle (e.g., Toyota).

model.description: The model of the vehicle (e.g., Vios).

year.description: The year the vehicle was manufactured.

plateNumber.description: The license plate number of the vehicle.

type.description: The type of vehicle (e.g., sedan, SUV).

PaymentMethod.title: PaymentMethod
PaymentMethod.description: Represents a payment method used within the Hatid application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the payment method.

details.description: The details of the payment method (e.g., card number, e-wallet ID).

LoyaltyProgram.title: LoyaltyProgram
LoyaltyProgram.description: Represents a loyalty program for riders within the Hatid application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the loyalty program.

name.description: The name of the loyalty program (e.g., 'Gold', 'Silver', 'Bronze').

description.description: A description of the loyalty program.

discountPercentage.description: The discount percentage offered by the loyalty program.

perks.description: Any perks offered by the loyalty program.

TrafficIncident.title: TrafficIncident
TrafficIncident.description: Represents a traffic incident reported by a user within the Hatid application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the traffic incident.

location.description: The location of the traffic incident (e.g., coordinates or address).

description.description: A description of the traffic incident.

reportedTime.description: The time the traffic incident was reported.

SuggestedPlace.title: SuggestedPlace
SuggestedPlace.description: Represents a suggested destination in Metro Manila.

id.description: Unique identifier for the suggested place.

location.description: The location of the place (e.g., coordinates or address).

rating.description: The average rating of the place.

imageURL.description: A link to an image of the place

AccessibilitySettings.title: AccessibilitySettings
AccessibilitySettings.description: Represents accessibility settings for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the accessibility settings.

voiceControlEnabled.description: Indicates whether voice control is enabled.

screenReaderEnabled.description: Indicates whether screen reader is enabled.

EmergencyContact.title: EmergencyContact
EmergencyContact.description: Represents an emergency contact for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the emergency contact.

name.description: The name of the emergency contact.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the emergency contact.

relationship.description: The relationship of the contact to the user (e.g., 'family', 'friend').

LostItem.title: LostItem
LostItem.description: Represents a lost item reported by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lost item.

description.description: A description of the lost item.

locationFound.description: The location where the item was found or lost.

reportTime.description: The time the item was reported lost.

status.description: The current status of the lost item (e.g., 'reported', 'found', 'returned').

SavedLocation.title: SavedLocation
SavedLocation.description: Represents a saved location for a rider.

id.description: Unique identifier for the saved location.

name.description: The name of the saved location (e.g., 'Home', 'Work').

address.description: The address of the saved location.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Includes userId as a parameter for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores driver-specific data associated with a user. Includes userId and driverId as parameters for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique identifier of the driver.  Matches the userId.

definition.description: Stores rider-specific data associated with a user. Includes userId and riderId as parameters for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: riderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the rider. Matches the userId.

definition.description: Stores trip data. Includes denormalized 'riderId' and 'driverId' for authorization independence.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier of the trip.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the vehicle.

definition.description: Stores payment methods for a user. Includes userId and paymentMethodId as parameters for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: paymentMethodId
params.description: The unique identifier of the payment method.

params.name: loyaltyProgramId
params.description: The unique identifier of the loyalty program.

params.name: trafficIncidentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the traffic incident.

params.name: suggestedPlaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the suggested place.

definition.description: Stores accessibility settings for a user. Includes userId and accessibilitySettingsId as parameters for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: accessibilitySettingsId
params.description: The unique identifier of the accessibility settings.  Should match userId.

definition.description: Stores emergency contacts for a user. Includes userId and emergencyContactId as parameters for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: emergencyContactId
params.description: The unique identifier of the emergency contact.

definition.description: Stores lost item reports for a user. Includes userId and lostItemId as parameters for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user. Should match the riderId on the document.

params.name: lostItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lost item.

definition.description: Stores saved locations for a user. Includes userId and savedLocationId as parameters for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user. Should match the riderId on the document.

params.name: savedLocationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the saved location.

definition.description: Collection used to store admin roles. The presence of a document indicates the user is an admin.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. It utilizes denormalization for authorization, structural segregation for homogeneous security postures, and consistent access modeling.  Specifically, user-owned data is stored in hierarchical paths under the `/users/{userId}` collection. Collaborative data (such as trips involving drivers and riders) denormalizes relevant authorization data (driverId, riderId) into each Trip document to avoid the need for `get()` calls in the security rules. Global roles are managed via existence checks in dedicated collections (e.g., /roles_admin/{uid}).

**Authorization Independence:** Achieved by denormalizing user roles and relationships directly into documents where those attributes are needed for authorization. For example, the `Trip` document includes both `driverId` and `riderId`, eliminating the need to fetch related User documents to determine authorization.

**QAPs Support:** The structure supports secure `list` operations by ensuring that collections contain documents with consistent security requirements (Structural Segregation).  Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/savedLocations/{savedLocationId}`) allows listing of a user's saved locations without complex filtering. Collections like `suggestedPlaces` can be queried and listed securely without user-specific filtering due to their public nature.

The decision to store Driver and Rider profiles as separate entities, referenced by the User ID, allows for simpler queries related to specific roles.  All user-specific data is namespaced under /users/{userId} to allow for easy management of user data.

The Trip entity uses a top-level collection to allow for easier global queries. Since both riderId and driverId are stored on the document, rules can be used to only allow Riders and Drivers to query Trips involving them without needing to check subcollections.
